title: MD
biography: Elizabeth Blackwell was the first woman to receive a medical degree in the United States (1849). She founded the New York Infirmary for Women and Children and was a pioneer of women in medicine.
aiSummary: Elizabeth Blackwell is a medicine specialist at New York Infirmary for Women and Children. Based in New York.

affiliations.hospitalName: New York Infirmary for Women and Children

timeline.title: First US female MD
timeline.description: Graduated from Geneva Medical College, becoming the first woman to earn an MD in America

timeline.title: Founded New York Infirmary
timeline.description: Established a hospital staffed entirely by women physicians

timeline.title: Founded Women's Medical College
timeline.description: Created a medical school for women in New York
